abstract: The base protocol for extensible file format support in Model I/O.
overview:
## Overview

By extending this protocol in your own custom protocols, you can define new functionality to add to the [doc://com.apple.modelio/documentation/ModelIO/MDLObject] instances in the object graph corresponding to a [doc://com.apple.modelio/documentation/ModelIO/MDLAsset] instance.

Model I/O adopts this pattern to support container objects (with the [doc://com.apple.modelio/documentation/ModelIO/MDLObjectContainerComponent] protocol) and objects with associated transformations (with the [doc://com.apple.modelio/documentation/ModelIO/MDLTransformComponent] protocol). To work with these aspects of a Model I/O object, call the object’s [doc://com.apple.modelio/documentation/ModelIO/MDLObject/componentConforming(to:)] method with the appropriate protocol. (The [doc://com.apple.modelio/documentation/ModelIO/MDLObject] class also provides convenience methods and properties for accessing features of these components without directly accessing the component instances.)

To add your own object features, first define a protocol that extends the [doc://com.apple.modelio/documentation/ModelIO/MDLComponent] protocol, implement a class that adopts that protocol, and then use the [doc://com.apple.modelio/documentation/ModelIO/MDLObject/setComponent(_:for:)] method to add your component to each object that needs it. The example below shows how you might implement a file format that associates scripting event triggers with parts of a game scene.

items.description: A default implementation for handling object hierarchy relationships in a 3D asset.
items.name: MDLObjectContainer

items.description: The general interface for classes that can act as containers in an object hierarchy.
items.name: MDLObjectContainerComponent

items.description: The general interface for classes that manage local coordinate space transforms for 3D objects
items.name: MDLTransformComponent

title: MDLComponent
